skill.name: industry-key-contact-radar-api-skill
skill.description: Discovers key contacts across industries, roles, and social platforms via the BrowserAct API. Extracts structured profile data including names, URLs, introductions, and company associations from search results on LinkedIn, Facebook, GitHub, and other sites.
skill.summary: Automates professional contact discovery and profile data extraction from social platforms using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 63 static analysis findings are false positives when evaluated in context. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to the BrowserAct API (network), reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using standard os.getenv() patterns (env_access), and documents bash execution commands in markdown code blocks (external_commands). The heuristic 'dangerous combination' finding is a false positive: reading an API key from env and using it for Bearer auth on a single documented API endpoint is a legitimate integration pattern. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent was detected. The skill is safe to publish as an API integration tool.

low_findings.title: HTTP requests to external API
low_findings.description: Python requests library usage to communicate with BrowserAct API endpoints. Legitimate usage for the skill's core function - no evidence of data exfiltration or unauthorized destinations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded service URLs
low_findings.description: URLs pointing to api.browseract.com and www.browseract.com for API calls and user guidance. All URLs are to the legitimate third-party service this skill integrates with.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API credentials
low_findings.description: Reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv(). Standard credential management pattern - key is not hardcoded, logged, or sent to unauthorized destinations.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash code blocks (```bash) showing example invocation commands. These are documentation examples, not executable Ruby backtick or shell injection commands. False positive from static pattern matching.

low_findings.title: Markdown text flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown text (feature descriptions, parameter docs, data field names) as cryptographic algorithm usage. No cryptographic code exists in either file - this is a text content false positive.

content.user_title: Find key industry contacts with BrowserAct Radar
content.value_statement: Finding key decision-makers across industries and platforms takes hours of manual searching. This skill automates contact discovery by extracting structured profile data from social platforms through the BrowserAct API.
actual_capabilities: Search for contacts by industry, job title, and target social platform
Extract structured profile data including URLs, names, introductions, and company associations
Discover decision-makers on LinkedIn, Facebook, and GitHub through a single command
Control result volume with configurable data limits from 1 to hundreds of records
Automate contact discovery workflows for lead generation and talent sourcing
Parse API responses into clean, actionable contact data without manual formatting
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Limited to the social platforms and data fields supported by the BrowserAct API template
Task execution may take several minutes depending on result volume and target platform
Each search accepts a single job title - multiple roles require separate queries

use_cases.title: B2B Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Build targeted prospect lists by searching for founders and CEOs in specific industries on LinkedIn or Facebook.

use_cases.title: Talent Sourcing
use_cases.description: Find candidates with specific job titles in target industries across multiple social platforms for recruiting.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Identify key personnel in competing organizations to understand team structures and hiring patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic contact search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find founders in the browser automation industry on Facebook.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user looking for a quick contact list with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Customized industry and role search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for marketing managers in the healthcare industry on LinkedIn. Return up to 20 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with specific targeting requirements and increased result volume

prompt_templates.title: Multi-platform talent discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find software engineers with the job title CTO in the fintech industry. Search on LinkedIn and GitHub. Collect profile URLs, names, and company information.
prompt_templates.scenario: User conducting cross-platform research for tech leadership candidates

prompt_templates.title: Competitive landscape analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a competitive analysis by searching for VP of Engineering in the e-commerce industry on LinkedIn. Extract company affiliations from the results to map competitor team structures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Business analyst researching competitor organization charts and hiring focus areas

output_examples.input: Find founders in the browser automation industry on Facebook.
output_examples.output: A list of profiles sorted by relevance, each showing: profile URL, name, brief bio introduction, and associated company. For example: John Doe - CEO of BrowserAutomation Inc. - facebook.com/johndoe - Building next-gen browser tools.

output_examples.input: Search for marketing managers in healthcare on LinkedIn with 15 results.
output_examples.output: 15 contact profiles displayed in order, each containing a direct LinkedIn profile link, full name, professional headline or introduction, and current employer or organization name.

output_examples.input: Find CTOs in the fintech industry on GitHub.
output_examples.output: A set of GitHub profile results showing usernames, profile introduction text, and repository or organization affiliations for CTO-level contacts in fintech.

best_practices: Verify the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the script, and ask the user for their key if it is missing.
Start with small data limits (10 results) to test platform coverage before scaling up to larger result sets.
Monitor the script output for error messages about invalid authorization or concurrent task limits, and guide the user to resolve them.
anti_patterns: Do not retry the script automatically more than once. If the second attempt fails, report the error to the user for manual investigation.
Do not ignore Invalid authorization errors by retrying. This indicates the API key is invalid and requires the user to provide a new key.
Do not modify the Python script or skip the API key check. The script must fail gracefully when the key is missing and prompt the user correctly.

faq.question: What is BrowserAct API?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a third-party service that provides automated browser workflows. This skill uses the Industry Key Contact Radar template from BrowserAct to discover professional contacts on social platforms.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a BrowserAct account and a valid API key stored in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Which social platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The skill can search on LinkedIn, Facebook, GitHub, and other platforms supported by the BrowserAct API template.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: Search times vary by result volume and platform. Most tasks complete within a few minutes. The script outputs status updates every 10 seconds so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: Can I search for multiple job titles at once?
faq.answer: Each search accepts a single job title. To search for multiple roles, run separate queries for each title.

faq.question: What data fields are returned for each contact?
faq.answer: Each result includes the contact profile URL, name, introduction or bio, and associated company or organization.
